title: The Whispered Secrets of Whitby Library
openingIncident: At 8:45 pm, Eleanor Whitmore, renowned antiquarian and curator of rare manuscripts, was found dead in the small study with a lamp, a book, a mug and a chair. Three people had been present during the final minutes, and each gave a different account. Nothing outside the room is needed to identify the killer.

suspects.name: Gareth Hargreaves
suspects.statement: I left at 8:30 pm. I never touched the lamp and did not go near the book.
suspects.alibiClaim: I had already left the room by 8:30 pm and did not return.

suspects.name: Clara Beaumont
suspects.statement: At 8:35 pm, Victor Lyle helped me move the chair clear of the doorway. We left the room together immediately afterwards. Gareth Hargreaves and Eleanor Whitmore were still inside.
suspects.alibiClaim: Victor Lyle and I were together outside the room from 8:35 pm until the discovery at 8:45 pm.

suspects.name: Victor Lyle
suspects.statement: At 8:35 pm, I helped Clara Beaumont move the chair clear of the doorway. We then left together and remained together until the body was found at 8:45 pm.
suspects.alibiClaim: Clara Beaumont and I were together outside the room from 8:35 pm until the discovery at 8:45 pm.

clues.title: The Dented Lamp
clues.detail: A fresh blood smear and a new impact dent mark the lamp. Eleanor Whitmore has a fresh blunt wound to the head.

clues.title: Movement of Chair
clues.detail: A fresh floor scrape made by the chair is crossed by a narrow blood droplet from the attack whose edge remains unbroken. Clara Beaumont and Victor Lyle both state that they moved the chair clear of the doorway at 8:35 pm before leaving together.

clues.title: Fibres on Lamp
clues.detail: Fine copper-coloured silk threads are caught inside a fresh split beneath the base of the lamp. Gareth Hargreaves's copper silk pocket square has a fresh narrow tear, exposing the same alternating copper and navy stripe.

clues.title: Fragment at Book
clues.detail: A curved black lacquer chip edged in gold is lodged between the cover and first page of the book on top of a narrow blood droplet from the attack. The black-and-gold fountain-pen cap worn by Gareth Hargreaves has a fresh curved gap in the lacquer; the exposed edges visibly fit the fragment.

clues.title: Crack in Mug
clues.detail: A crack runs across the mug. A continuous yellowed glue line is visible deep inside the break.

solution.motive: Gareth Hargreaves killed Eleanor Whitmore because the imminent exposure of a forged medieval manuscript that would ruin his reputation.
solution.method: Eleanor Whitmore was killed by a heavy blow from the lamp.
solution.timeOfDeath: After 8:35 pm and before discovery at 8:45 pm
solution.opportunity: Gareth Hargreaves claimed to have left at 8:30 pm. The timeline evidence places the attack after Clara Beaumont and Victor Lyle left together at 8:35 pm; the fine copper-coloured silk threads on the lamp and the black and gold fragment recovered from the book place Gareth Hargreaves inside afterwards. Gareth Hargreaves therefore had the only evidenced opportunity to be alone with Eleanor Whitmore and reach the lamp during the murder window.
